top_categories.workout_context: 152ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Gran Fondo & Ultra Raid Samoëns, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
16,404ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo & Ultra Raid Samoëns' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
At mile 60 of 108, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
16,404ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo & Ultra Raid Samoëns. Train it until it feels boring.
Gran Fondo & Ultra Raid Samoëns' mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 108 miles.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 108 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Low-cadence force work for 16,404ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 16,404ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 108 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo & Ultra Raid Samoëns’s 108 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Gran Fondo & Ultra Raid Samoëns with 16,404ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Gran Fondo & Ultra Raid Samoëns requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Over Under, and VO2max to prepare you for 108 miles of mixed terrain in Samoëns, Haute-Savoie, France.
